content_markdown:
A container holds $20\text{ g}$ of neon (mass number $20$) and also $8\text{ g}$ of helium (mass number $4$).

What is the ratio $\frac{\text{number of atoms of neon}}{\text{number of atoms of helium}}$?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the image.

vibe_explanation: To find the ratio of the number of atoms, we first find the number of moles for each gas. For neon, $n_{\text{Ne}} = \frac{20\text{ g}}{20\text{ g/mol}} = 1\text{ mole}$. For helium, $n_{\text{He}} = \frac{8\text{ g}}{4\text{ g/mol}} = 2\text{ moles}$. Since the number of atoms is proportional to the number of moles ($N = n \times N_A$), the ratio of atoms is the same as the ratio of moles: $1 / 2 = 0.5$.
